chapter: Chapter 1: What is Democracy? Why Democracy?

questions.question: Which combination correctly represents the four features of democracy?

options.A: Elected rulers, free elections, political equality, rule of law
options.C: Single party rule, monarchy, equality, dictatorship
options.D: Military rule, dictatorship, monarchy, hereditary rule

questions.explanation: These four are the essential features of democracy.

questions.question: Which situation best represents democracy?

options.B: People elect leaders who make decisions

questions.explanation: Democracy involves elected representatives chosen by citizens.

questions.question: Which of the following indicates lack of democratic elections?

options.B: Candidates chosen by one political party only

questions.explanation: Limited candidate choice weakens democracy.

questions.question: Which democratic principle ensures fairness in political participation?

options.A: One person one vote one value

questions.explanation: This principle ensures political equality.

questions.question: Which statement best explains democracy as accountable government?

options.B: Leaders are answerable to citizens through elections

questions.explanation: Regular elections make leaders accountable.

questions.question: Which feature allows peaceful change of rulers in democracy?
questions.explanation: Elections allow citizens to replace leaders peacefully.

questions.question: Why is democracy considered better for decision making?
questions.explanation: Discussion reduces mistakes in decisions.

questions.question: Which democratic value helps resolve conflicts?
questions.explanation: Democracy resolves conflicts through peaceful discussion.

questions.question: Which feature of democracy enhances dignity of citizens?
questions.explanation: Democracy treats citizens as equal participants.

questions.question: How does democracy allow correction of mistakes?
questions.explanation: Citizens can replace rulers through elections.

questions.question: Which country example shows military dominance despite elections?
questions.explanation: Pakistan under General Musharraf had military influence.

questions.question: Which country required Communist Party approval for candidates?
questions.explanation: China allows elections but with limited candidate choice.

questions.question: Which country had one-party dominance for decades?
questions.explanation: Mexico had a dominant party for many years.

questions.question: Which system involves citizens electing representatives?
questions.explanation: People elect representatives to govern.

questions.question: Why is representative democracy necessary?
questions.explanation: Large populations make direct democracy impractical.

questions.question: Which democratic feature limits government power?
questions.explanation: The constitution sets limits on government authority.

questions.question: Which value helps democracy manage social diversity?
questions.explanation: Consultation helps different groups coexist peacefully.

questions.question: Which system promotes equality in political rights?
questions.explanation: All adults have the right to vote.

questions.question: Which democratic value strengthens citizen participation?
questions.explanation: Freedom of expression allows citizens to voice opinions.

questions.question: Which system allows peaceful coexistence in diverse societies?
questions.explanation: Democracy allows discussion among diverse groups.

questions.question: Which factor strengthens democracy the most?
questions.explanation: Participation strengthens democracy.

questions.question: Which democratic principle ensures fairness in elections?
questions.explanation: Political equality ensures equal voting rights.

questions.question: Which feature ensures leaders follow legal limits?
questions.explanation: Rule of law limits government power.

questions.question: Which democratic value promotes better decision making?
questions.explanation: Discussion and consultation improve decisions.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes democracy?

options.A: People elect rulers and participate in governance

questions.explanation: Democracy is based on elections and citizen participation.
